items.q: Which of these seas is almost enclosed by Europe and Asia and connected to the Mediterranean through the Bosphorus and Dardanelles?
items.explanation: The Black Sea connects to the Mediterranean through a chain of narrow waterways including the Bosphorus and Dardanelles.

items.q: Indonesia is spread across more than 17,000 islands.
items.explanation: Indonesia is the world's largest archipelagic state and stretches across a wide span of Southeast Asia.

items.q: The Equator passes through mainland Australia.
items.explanation: Australia lies entirely south of the Equator, while the line passes north of it through countries such as Indonesia.

items.q: What is the name of the major rift system that runs through eastern Africa?
items.explanation: This rift system extends from the Levant into eastern Africa and reflects ongoing tectonic movement.

items.q: Which US state borders both the Pacific Ocean and Mexico?
items.explanation: California shares an international border with the Mexican state of Baja California and has a long Pacific coastline.

items.q: Which lake lies on the border between Peru and Bolivia?
items.explanation: Lake Titicaca sits high in the Andes and is often noted as the highest commercially navigable lake in the world.

items.q: What is a basin in physical geography?
options: A basin is a broad low area of land where water drains and collects.
An isthmus is a narrow strip of land connecting two larger landmasses.
A fjord is a steep-sided coastal inlet carved by glaciers.
A mesa is a flat-topped elevated landform with steep sides.
items.a: A basin is a broad low area of land where water drains and collects.
items.explanation: Drainage basins are defined by the area where precipitation flows toward a common river, lake, or inland outlet.

items.q: Which landlocked country borders Germany, Austria, Slovakia, and Poland?
items.explanation: The Czech Republic sits in central Europe and has no coastline despite bordering several nearby states.

items.q: What best describes the tundra biome?
options: A cold treeless biome with permafrost and a short growing season
A hot desert biome with year-round drought and shifting dunes
A temperate grassland biome dominated by deep-rooted prairie vegetation
A tropical forest biome with heavy rainfall and a dense canopy
items.a: A cold treeless biome with permafrost and a short growing season
items.explanation: Tundra environments are common in the Arctic and at very high elevations where soils remain frozen for much of the year.

items.q: What ancient city in modern-day Jordan is famous for buildings carved into rose-red rock?
items.explanation: Petra was the capital of the Nabataean Kingdom and is one of the best-known archaeological sites in the Middle East.
